Clause / Topic: Employer free-issue reinforcement steel delay - primary TIA clause
Location: Clause 4.20 / Appendix advance payment steel supply / Clause 8.4 / Clause 20.1
Plain English Meaning: Employer-supplied reinforcement must align with the approved programme and activity need dates. Late, short, or unusable supply can support EOT only when readiness and critical-path impact are proven.
Research the Lines: Search keywords: free issue steel, reinforcement, RFT, employer supplied material, approved programme, stock out, TIA, critical path, delivery note, material requisition.
Who Holds Leverage: Shared - Contractor holds leverage where readiness, shortage, notice, and TIA impact are complete.
Notice / Time Bar: Immediate notice and monthly particulars with activity ID, need date, shortage quantity, delivery recovery, and impacted update.
Money Impact: Potential prolongation and disruption if non-concurrent Employer risk is proven.
Schedule Impact: Strong EOT candidate where shortage delays longest path or contractual milestone.
Practical Action / Evidence: Programme updates; steel requisitions; delivery notes; stock ledger; MIRs; daily reports; photos; TIA fragnet; concurrency matrix.

Clause / Topic: IFC / design conflict and delayed technical release
Plain English Meaning: Conflicting or late IFC information can delay engineering, procurement, and construction where the Contractor cannot reasonably proceed without approved resolution.
Research the Lines: Search keywords: IFC conflict, shop drawing, design clarification, RFI, revised drawings, slab opening, column clash, approval delay.
Who Holds Leverage: Contractor if the hold point is mandatory and submitted records are timely.
Notice / Time Bar: Notify upon discovery and update once final instruction or revised IFC is issued.
Money Impact: May support prolongation cost when the delay is compensable and non-concurrent.
Schedule Impact: Supports EOT if it controls a critical engineering-to-construction path.
Practical Action / Evidence: RFI log; transmittals; comments; revised IFC drawings; hold point photos; activity readiness proof.

Location: Payment clause / certification procedure / delayed payment interest
Plain English Meaning: Delayed certification or payment may create commercial and recovery constraints but must be separately proven from pure time entitlement.
Research the Lines: Search keywords: invoice, certified amount, paid amount, interest, cash cheque, release period, payment delay, recovery resources.
Who Holds Leverage: Shared - depends on contract entitlement and causal link to progress.
Notice / Time Bar: Submit payment notices, interest calculation, and mitigation records.
Money Impact: Direct interest and possible financing/prolongation impact if substantiated.
Schedule Impact: Normally supportive unless delay directly controls resource procurement on critical path.
Practical Action / Evidence: Invoices; certificates; bank receipts; cash-flow records; procurement commitments; recovery plan.
